step.title: Load Bundle
step.goal: Read the entire bundle into working memory plus the AGENT-BRIEF principles reference. ticket-write is the single point where the ticket body's content gets written; it needs everything in head.

substep.title: Locate the Bundle
substep.ask:
Which ticket bundle should I write?
Paste the bundle directory path or the ticket file path. The bundle should have these prior-phase sections in the WORKLOG: - Q↔R Readiness Summary (from ticket-grill) - Design Summary (from ticket-design) - Structure Outline (from ticket-structure) - Writing Plan (from ticket-plan)
If any is missing, run the prior phase first.
substep.action: Validate. Halt if prior-phase sections are missing.

substep.title: Read Bundle Files
substep.action:
Read the ticket, research, questions, and worklog files completely. Extract: - Primary tag, slug - All prior-phase sections (Readiness, Design, Structure, Plan) from the worklog - Research findings (synthesized findings from ticket-grill turns, including any prototype findings) - Q&A entries (resolved questions; rejected alternatives) - Linked DRs (read each DR's content; the ticket will reference them in its body)
Store in session: primary_tag, slug, design_summary, structure_outline, writing_plan, research_findings, qa_entries, locked_drs, bundle_dir, ticket_path, etc.

substep.title: Load AGENT-BRIEF Principles
substep.action: Read {{base_dir}}/references/agent-brief-principles.md completely. This reference defines the discipline every section drafted in step 3 must honor. Store the seven principles + the section-quality checklist into session memory for use during iteration.

substep.title: Display Load Summary
